title: NDIC Addresses Allegations Of Financial Impropriety Involving Staff Member
description: The Nigeria Deposit Insurance Corporation (NDIC) said it will address the allegations of financial impropriety involving its staff member. This was contained in a statement that was made available to
html:
The Nigeria Deposit Insurance Corporation (NDIC) said it will address the allegations of financial impropriety involving its staff member. This was contained in a statement that was made available to Financial Edge at the weekend.

The statement which was signed by the Director, Communications & Public Affairs Department, Bashir Nuhu, noted that “The attention of the Management of the Nigeria Deposit Insurance Corporation (NDIC) has been drawn to the media report by the Peoples’ Gazette and Sahara Reporters regarding allegations of financial impropriety involving one of our staff members”

According to him “We would like to emphasize that while we are assessing the veracity of the reported incident, the alleged events are unrelated to the operations of the Corporation. Nonetheless, as a responsible Federal Institution, we are committed to uncovering the truth and addressing the matter in an appropriate manner”.

Adding that “following our preliminary investigations into the allegations, we have discovered that the staff member in question was previously employed at a commercial bank as an account officer to the Federal Government Official referenced in the report before joining the NDIC in 2017. It is important to note that during her tenure at the bank, she asserts that she was not involved in any improper financial transactions with anyone”.

Stressing that “however, we remain diligent in our pursuit of any contradictory information and will not hesitate to take appropriate action should it arise concerning the staff member involved. The NDIC maintains a zero-tolerance policy towards financial impropriety and any actions that contravene our core values, corporate culture, and code of conduct”.

While assuring that “we wish to emphasize that the NDIC is committed to upholding the highest ethical standards in our corporate governance practices, which we have diligently cultivated over the past three decades of our existence in fulfilling our role of depositor protection and contributing to financial system stability”.
